title: Crepidomanes makinoi
taxonomy: Crepidomanes makinoi is a species of fern in the family Hymenophyllaceae. It belongs to the genus Crepidomanes and the phylum Pteridophyta. The classification of Crepidomanes makinoi places it within the kingdom Plantae, specifically within the division Pteridophyta. This species is a type of delicate, epiphytic fern that is native to certain regions. Further classification may include order Hymenophyllales and class Polypodiopsida.

intro: Crepidomanes makinoi is a species of fern native to Japan, belonging to the family Hymenophyllaceae, commonly known as the filmy ferns or air plants. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its purported health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Crepidomanes makinoi include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Crepidomanes makinoi is often prepared as a tea or infusions, as well as in the form of powders or capsules, and is used to alleviate symptoms of coughs, colds, and bronchitis.
